output.title: Implement Exponential Backoff and Dead-Letter Queue for Payment Webhooks
output.summary: This pull request introduces a retry policy for processing payment webhooks, implementing exponential backoff for transient failures and ensuring failed events are sent to a dead-letter queue.
changes: Added exponential backoff retry logic to the payment webhook handler.
Implemented dead-letter queue handling for failed payment events.
testPlan: Test that the webhook handler retries processing up to 5 times for transient failures.
Verify that failed events are correctly published to the dead-letter queue.
Ensure that retry metrics are emitted for observability.
Check that no duplicate charges occur when replaying events.
risks: Potential for increased latency in processing if retries are triggered frequently.
Need to ensure that the dead-letter queue is monitored and processed appropriately to avoid data loss.
output.rolloutNotes: Monitor the performance of the webhook handler after deployment to ensure that the retry logic does not introduce significant delays during peak traffic.

output.title: Add retry policy for payment webhook processing
output.summary: This pull request implements a retry policy for processing payment webhooks, addressing intermittent failures during peak traffic. It introduces exponential backoff retries and dead-letter handling for failed events.
changes: Added exponential backoff retry mechanism for payment webhook processing.
Implemented dead-letter queue handling for failed events.
testPlan: Verify that the webhook handler retries transient failures up to 5 times.
Ensure that failed events are correctly persisted to the dead-letter queue.
Check that retry metrics are emitted for observability.
Test that no duplicate charge side effects occur on replay of events.
risks: Potential for increased latency in processing if retries are triggered frequently.
Need to ensure that the dead-letter queue is monitored and processed to avoid data loss.
output.rolloutNotes: Monitor the performance and reliability of the webhook processing after deployment, especially during peak traffic periods.
